executive_summary: No P0 or P1 issue was found. Remaining items are P2/P3 promotion and assurance follow-ups around release proof, sdist smoke testing, CLI onboarding clarity, broader secret scanning, and finance-boundary preservation.

verification.name: wheel_smoke
verification.command: python -m venv /tmp/pfds_review_smoke && /tmp/pfds_review_smoke/bin/python -m pip install /tmp/pfds_review_dist/portfolio_fee_drag_simulator-1.0.1-py3-none-any.whl --no-deps && /tmp/pfds_review_smoke/bin/portfolio-fee-drag --version && /tmp/pfds_review_smoke/bin/python -m portfolio_fee_drag_simulator selfcheck

findings.title: Reproducibility path is strong, but release proof is still local and transcript-based
findings.risk: Public reviewers can reproduce locally, but the tag should include a fresh human-readable transcript rather than relying only on generated JSON.
follow_up_items: Regenerate demo/release_manifest.json after the final commit.
Attach a release transcript showing tests, selfcheck, quickstart, public scan, wheel build, and wheel smoke.
Keep the Python >=3.11 requirement visible in release notes.

findings.title: Package install looks good for wheel, but sdist was not independently smoked
findings.risk: Wheel installation passed, but source distributions exercise a different archive path.
follow_up_items: Build and install an sdist in a clean venv before publishing.
Smoke both wheel and sdist with --no-deps.

findings.title: CLI discoverability is complete but dense
findings.risk: The CLI exposes 29 commands, which can make the public first-run path harder to see.
follow_up_items: Lead release notes with install, quickstart-check, demo/showcase.html, tests, selfcheck, and public-scan.
Consider future docs-only grouping into first-run, analysis, and release-owner command sections.

findings.title: Finance boundaries are prominent, but promotion copy must not overstate the output
findings.risk: Dollar estimates, rankings, and risk-flag labels could look like personalized investment analysis if copied without the boundary.
follow_up_items: Include no-live-data, no-broker, no-advice, and no-recommendation language in release notes and screenshots.
Avoid promotion phrases implying optimization, recommendations, or tax-aware advice.
Keep scenario wording tied to deterministic bundled or user-supplied assumptions.

findings.title: No-network/security posture passes current checks, but scanner depth is limited
findings.risk: Built-in scanning is intentionally simple and does not replace entropy or git-history secret scanning.
follow_up_items: Run gitleaks or trufflehog before tagging.
Include git history scanning if private data may ever have been committed.
Consider future AST-based import scanning for security-boundary-report.

findings.title: Demo artifact integrity is good, with two scope limits to document
findings.risk: The catalog and visual receipt are useful review artifacts, but they are not a cryptographic release attestation.
follow_up_items: Use demo/release_manifest.json as the broad release hash inventory.
Use demo/visual_receipt.json as selected visual evidence only.
Regenerate quickstart artifacts after any docs or demo changes, then rerun public scan.

findings.title: Promotion readiness is pass-with-manual-proof
findings.risk: Generated checklist output still depends on human release-owner judgment for manual wheel install proof.
follow_up_items: Attach wheel smoke output or summarize it in release notes.
Do not set release-audit-summary tests-status pass unless tests were run after the final commit.
Keep source behavior unchanged for v1.0.1 unless a true P0/P1 appears.

release_owner_follow_up_checklist: Regenerate demo artifacts with python -m portfolio_fee_drag_simulator quickstart-check --output demo.
Build wheel and sdist, then smoke both in clean venvs.
Run an external secret scanner before tagging.
Include no-advice/no-live-data boundaries in release notes and screenshots.
